title: Overtime pay

meta.description: Find out about overtime pay, when it applies, and how TOIL (time off in lieu) works.

headings.text: Time off instead of overtime pay (TOIL)

content:
Overtime is time worked beyond an employee's ordinary hours of work. When overtime applies, and what rate is paid, depends on the employee's award or registered agreement.

When does overtime apply?
Overtime generally applies when an employee works:
- More than their ordinary daily hours (commonly more than 7.6 or 8 hours per day)
- More than their ordinary weekly hours (commonly more than 38 hours per week)
- Outside their spread of hours as defined in their award
- On days they are not ordinarily required to work

Common overtime rates set by awards:
- First 2–3 hours of overtime: 150% (time and a half) of the ordinary rate
- Beyond that: 200% (double time) of the ordinary rate
- Public holiday overtime (SCHADS Award): 250% (double time and a half)

Time off instead of overtime (TOIL):
Some awards allow an employer and employee to agree to TOIL instead of overtime pay. Under TOIL arrangements:
- The employee must agree in writing
- The employee takes time off equal to the overtime worked (at the applicable overtime rate)
- TOIL must be taken within a reasonable period
- The agreement cannot disadvantage the employee

Maximum weekly hours:
Full-time employees' ordinary hours are 38 per week. Additional hours beyond 38 per week may be 'reasonable additional hours' under the NES (see s.62 Fair Work Act). Whether an employee must be paid overtime for those hours depends on their award or agreement.

Salaried employees:
Some awards and agreements include overtime provisions for salaried employees. From 16 September 2023, employees under the Professional Employees Award 2020 are entitled to overtime pay for working more than the hours specified in the award.

Casual employees:
Casual employees may also be entitled to overtime rates under their award — always check the applicable award clause.

Legislative basis: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth), s.62 (maximum weekly hours); individual Modern Award overtime clauses.
tables: Equivalent time off at overtime rate
